---
title: "Welcome"
---

::: {.hero-banner}
# Python for Beginners

A friendly, practical course for learning real Python by building small programs, solving exercises, and making fun projects.
:::

## Start here

This course is designed for complete beginners. You do not need to know any coding before you begin.

You will learn by:

- reading short explanations,
- typing code yourself,
- solving exercises,
- opening hints only when needed,
- checking full solutions only after trying properly,
- and building small projects.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Best way to learn

Type the examples yourself instead of copying and pasting. Programming is learned through practice.
:::

## What you will learn

By the end of the first five lessons, you will be able to:

- write and run Python programs,
- print text and numbers,
- use variables,
- ask the user for input,
- make decisions with `if`,
- repeat code with loops,
- and work with lists.

## Course map

::: {.course-grid}

::: {.course-card}
### 1. Setup

Install Python, open Positron, and learn the main parts of the coding window.
:::

::: {.course-card}
### 2. First programs

Use `print()`, variables, strings and arithmetic.
:::

::: {.course-card}
### 3. Interactive programs

Use `input()` to ask questions and respond to the user's answers.
:::

::: {.course-card}
### 4. Decisions

Use `if`, `else` and `elif` to make your programs choose between options.
:::

::: {.course-card}
### 5. Loops

Use `while` and `for` loops so your programs can repeat actions.
:::

::: {.course-card}
### 6. Projects

Build games, calculators, quizzes and other small programs.
:::

:::

## Suggested pace

A good pace is **one lesson per session**, with 45–60 minutes per lesson.

For each lesson:

1. Read the explanation.
2. Run the examples.
3. Try the exercises.
4. Open hints only when needed.
5. Check the solutions at the end.
6. Try at least one challenge.

## Ready?

Start with [Setup](setup.qmd).
